introduction: A common cause of persistent sore throat in young and most middle-aged adults, usually unaccompanied by other symptoms. Often secondary to chronic nasal conditions with nasal obstruction e.g. Vasomotor rhinitis, Nasal polyps, Septal deviation. Obstruction causes mouth breathing with dryness of the throat. Other causes include secondary inflammation from postnasal discharge of sinusitis, chronic exposure to irritants such as tobacco smoke and alcohol, and secondary infection from carious teeth.

features: Persistent sore throat with no systemic upset or dysphagia
Sore throat is often worse in the mornings

investigations: Throat swab: microscopy, culture and sensitivity

goals: Control symptoms by identifying and treating primary causes
supportive_measures: Reduction or avoidance of exposure to known irritants-tobacco, alcohol, etc

complications: More often related to the primary sources of irritation or infection
